claim: FedProTrack re-ID advantage over CFL requires T>=20 on CIFAR-100 because the Gibbs posterior needs >=10 federation rounds to build informative transition priors; at T=6 the posterior has only 3 update opportunities and kappa=0.6 stickiness cannot disambiguate concepts
experiment: Run CIFAR-100 recurrence with K=4, n_samples=200, seed=[42,43,44,45,46], T in [6,10,15,20,30,40]. Methods: FedProTrack, CFL, IFCA, FedAvg. Track re-ID accuracy, assignment_entropy trajectory, and posterior convergence (fraction of rounds where MAP probability > 0.8). Budget-matched comparison at each T.
pass_criterion: FedProTrack re-ID exceeds CFL re-ID by >= 0.05 at some T >= 20 while remaining below CFL at T <= 10. Assignment entropy should decrease monotonically with T for FedProTrack but not for CFL. Posterior convergence rate (MAP > 0.8) should exceed 0.7 at the winning T.
narrative: Memory helps when the server has enough temporal observations to build confident concept identity estimates. At short horizons the Gibbs posterior is under-determined and memory tracking overhead is wasted -- simpler clustering (CFL) dominates. This establishes a principled minimum-horizon condition for identity-based federation.

claim: Posterior collapse in hybrid-proto mode is caused by a feedback loop: prototype_alignment_mix modifies aggregated models to resemble fingerprint class-means, making all concept fingerprints converge and the Gibbs posterior collapse to a single assignment. Decoupling fingerprint computation from prototype alignment recovers re-ID.
experiment: Run CIFAR-100 recurrence at K=4, T=20, n_samples=400, seeds=[42,43,44,45,46]. Compare 4 FedProTrack variants: (A) base (prototype_alignment_mix=0.0), (B) hybrid-proto (prototype_alignment_mix=0.3), (C) feature_adapter fingerprint with prototype_alignment_mix=0.3 (fingerprint_source=pre_adapter_embed, model_type=feature_adapter), (D) delayed alignment (prototype_alignment_mix=0.3, prototype_alignment_early_rounds=5, prototype_alignment_early_mix=0.0). Measure re-ID, final_acc, assignment_entropy per round, and inter-concept fingerprint similarity.
pass_criterion: Variant C or D achieves re-ID >= 0.55 AND final_acc >= 0.55 while maintaining assignment_entropy > 0.1. Variant B assignment_entropy < 0.01 and inter-concept fingerprint similarity > 0.95 confirming the feedback loop. Winning variant inter-concept similarity < 0.85.
narrative: Memory hurts when model-sharing mechanisms destroy the concept identity signal that memory relies on. Prototype alignment improves accuracy but when coupled to fingerprinting features it erases distributional differences the Gibbs posterior needs. Decoupling these functions lets FedProTrack get both prototype-aware sharing AND reliable identity tracking.

claim: On CIFAR-100 (20 classes), concept specialization via memory retrieval is counterproductive below ~500 samples/step because concept-specific models overfit; FedProTrack final_acc should cross over CFL only when n_samples provides >=25 samples per class.
experiment: Run CIFAR-100 recurrence at K=4, T=20, seeds=[42,43,44,45,46], n_samples in [100, 200, 400, 800, 1200]. Methods: FedProTrack, FedAvg, IFCA, CFL, Oracle. Track final_acc and re-ID at each n_samples level. Log per-concept train-test accuracy gap as overfitting proxy.
pass_criterion: Crossover point n_samples* exists in [400, 800] where FedProTrack final_acc overtakes CFL. Below n_samples* FedProTrack final_acc < CFL. Oracle and FedProTrack train-test gap > 0.15 at n_samples=200 but < 0.08 at n_samples=800.
narrative: Memory helps only when per-concept data suffices for reliable model specialization. When samples are scarce relative to class complexity the retrieved concept-specific models are overfit and harmful -- pooling across concepts wins. This identifies a data-efficiency boundary for identity-based federation and motivates regularization for the next iteration.

claim: On CIFAR-100 with concept-specific label distributions (disjoint or overlap), concept identity tracking should produce accuracy gains because the correct model depends on which classes are present.
experiment: Phase 1: 3-way split (none/overlap/disjoint) K=4 T=12 n_samples=200, 3 seeds. Phase 2: threshold tuning sweep (10 configs, 3 seeds). Phase 3: 5-seed definitive comparison on disjoint.
pass_criterion: Oracle accuracy increases >15% from none to disjoint. FedProTrack accuracy gap vs CFL decreases or reverses on disjoint.
narrative: PARTIALLY VALIDATED. Dataset design confirmed: Oracle 0.42->0.852 on disjoint. FPT beats FedAvg (+2%: 0.717 vs 0.697) but trails CFL by 9.5% (0.717 vs 0.812). Root cause: over-spawning (6 concepts for 4 true, no merging). Re-ID ceiling ~0.63 regardless of threshold tuning. CFL's model-weight clustering is structurally richer than fingerprint routing. Communication-efficiency reframing may be the stronger argument.

claim: Under jointly favorable conditions (K=10, T=30, n_samples=800, disjoint labels, rho=7.5 giving 4 concepts, alpha=0.5), FedProTrack closes the accuracy gap to CFL and achieves re-ID >= 0.65.
experiment: run_fpt_advantage.py (5 seeds) + run_phase_diagram.py (3 splits x 3 seeds). Low-singleton matrix (min_group_size=2) to eliminate singleton confound.
pass_criterion: FPT final_acc within 5% of CFL on disjoint. FPT re-ID >= 0.65.
result_summary: REFUTED. FPT 0.682 vs CFL 0.788 (gap=10.5%). FPT even trails FedAvg (0.734) by 5.2%. Re-ID achieved 0.739 (pass), but 27% misattribution rate causes each wrong assignment to route samples to a model trained on entirely different classes. Misattribution cost model: expected acc = 0.73*0.80 + 0.27*0.0 = 0.584, matching observed 0.588. Over-spawning is NOT the cause (tightening max_concepts to 4 gives identical accuracy). Oracle matches CFL (0.789 vs 0.788), confirming concept-awareness value at 100% re-ID.

claim: Oracle matches or exceeds CFL on disjoint-label CIFAR-100 concepts when properly tuned.
result_summary: VALIDATED. Oracle 0.789 vs CFL 0.788 (K=10, T=30, 5 seeds, min_group_size=2, disjoint labels). Concept-awareness ceiling is real.

claim: Low-singleton matrix post-processing (min_group_size=2) eliminates singleton groups without destroying recurrence.
result_summary: VALIDATED. Singleton ratio drops from 42% to 0% across all 5 seeds. Concept distribution remains balanced.

claim: Posterior-gated fallback: when FedProTrack's assignment entropy exceeds a threshold (indicating uncertain concept identity), falling back to a global FedAvg model should improve accuracy by avoiding misattribution cost. Breakeven re-ID for disjoint labels is ~0.79; FPT achieves 0.73, so uncertain assignments should use the global model.
experiment: Implement uncertainty_fallback_threshold in soft aggregation. Sweep threshold in [0.5, 0.8, 1.0, 1.2]. Compare FPT-fallback vs FPT-base vs FedAvg on disjoint K=10 T=30 n_samples=800, 5 seeds.
pass_criterion: FPT-fallback achieves final_acc >= 0.73 (matching or exceeding FedAvg) while maintaining re-ID >= 0.65.
narrative: The H5 misattribution cost analysis shows the core problem: with disjoint labels, wrong concept routing is catastrophically bad. A simple fix is to not route when uncertain. This preserves re-ID capability (still tracked by the posterior) while avoiding the accuracy penalty of misrouting.

claim: CFL's ~10% accuracy advantage over FedProTrack on CIFAR-100 is NOT caused by CFL's clustering algorithm. CFL in this setting is effectively FedAvg with n_epochs=10 (its splitting criterion never triggers). The gap decomposes as: (1) n_epochs mismatch (CFL=10 vs FPT=1) accounts for ~13pp, (2) FPT's accurate concept routing HURTS accuracy via over-fragmentation (splitting 10 clients into ~10 groups = no aggregation benefit) accounts for ~13pp, (3) Gibbs machinery overhead ~3-5pp.
experiment: run_cfl_ablation.py: 14 method variants across 3 seeds (42,123,456). CFL-original, CFL-fair-split (50/50 eval), CFL-fair-1ep (1 epoch), CFL-no-warmup (warmup=0), FedAvg, FedAvg-10ep, FPT-base, FPT-10ep, FPT-10ep-blend05, FPT-update-fp, FPT-update-fp-10ep, FPT-calibrated, FPT-hybrid-10ep, Oracle.
pass_criterion: CFL-fair-split approx equals CFL-original (data split <2pp effect). CFL-fair-1ep drops >10pp (training strength is primary). FedAvg-10ep >= CFL-fair-split (confirming CFL clustering is decorative). FPT-10ep still trails FedAvg-10ep (confirming over-fragmentation).
result_summary: VALIDATED. CFL-original=0.619 vs CFL-fair-split=0.621 (data split: +0.002, nil). CFL-fair-1ep=0.494 (epochs: -0.127, massive). CFL-no-warmup=0.621 (warmup: 0pp). FedAvg-10ep=0.643 > CFL=0.619 (clustering decorative, confirmed). FPT-10ep=0.509 < FedAvg-10ep=0.643 (over-fragmentation: -0.134, confirmed). FPT-base re-ID=0.769 but acc=0.443 -- accurate routing destroys accuracy via singleton groups.
